Test_Objective: Verify the department dropdown opens when user clicks the dropdown trigger
Precondition: User is logged in and on a page containing the department dropdown
Steps:
1. Navigate to the page with the department dropdown.
2. Click on the department dropdown trigger.
Expected_Result: Dropdown list expands showing department items with dark background, rounded corners. Currently selected department is highlighted.

Test_Objective: Verify the department dropdown opens when user presses Enter or Space on focused trigger
Precondition: User is logged in and on a page containing the department dropdown
Steps:
1. Navigate to the page with the department dropdown.
2. Tab to focus on the dropdown trigger.
3. Press Enter (or Space).
Expected_Result: Dropdown list expands showing department items.

Test_Objective: Verify the dropdown closes after selecting a department
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Click on any department item.
Expected_Result: Dropdown closes. Selected department is applied as filter.

Test_Objective: Verify the dropdown closes when user clicks outside the dropdown area
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Click on any area outside the dropdown.
Expected_Result: Dropdown closes. Previously selected department remains unchanged.

Test_Objective: Verify the dropdown closes when user presses the Escape key
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Press the Escape key.
Expected_Result: Dropdown closes. Previously selected department remains unchanged.

Test_Objective: Verify dropdown behaves correctly on double-click
Precondition: User is logged in and on a page containing the department dropdown
Steps: 1. Double-click on the department dropdown trigger.
Expected_Result: Dropdown opens and closes (or opens once) without error. No duplicate actions.

Test_Objective: Verify the dropdown container displays correctly per design specs
Precondition: User is logged in and dropdown is open
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Observe the dropdown container.
Expected_Result: Dropdown container displays with dark background, rounded corners, approximately 101x348px dimensions.

Test_Objective: Verify the dropdown appears in correct position relative to the trigger element
Steps:
1. Click on the department dropdown trigger.
2. Observe the position of the dropdown list.
Expected_Result: Dropdown list appears directly below (or near) the trigger element, properly aligned.

Test_Objective: Verify all department items have centered text
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Observe text alignment of all visible items.
Expected_Result: All department names are centered within their respective list items.

Test_Objective: Verify unselected items display white text on dark background
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Observe text color of unselected items.
Expected_Result: Unselected items display white text on dark background.

Test_Objective: Verify consistent spacing between department items
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Observe spacing between consecutive items.
Expected_Result: All items have consistent, uniform spacing between them.

Test_Objective: Verify the default selected department is displayed correctly when dropdown opens
Precondition: User is on the page, no department filter change has been made
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Observe the initially selected/highlighted item.
Expected_Result: The default department (e.g. user's own department) is highlighted with a lighter background.

Test_Objective: Verify visible departments display correctly in the initial view
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Observe the visible items without scrolling.
Expected_Result: Approximately 6 department items are visible (CEVC2, CEVC3, CEVC4, CEVC1, OPD, Infra as shown in design). Text is readable and not truncated.

Test_Objective: Verify the selected department item has a distinct highlighted appearance
Precondition: Dropdown is open with a department selected
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Observe the currently selected department item.
Expected_Result: Selected item displays with a lighter/brighter background, clearly distinguishable from unselected items.

Test_Objective: Verify only one department can be visually highlighted as selected
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Count the number of items with selected/highlight state.
Expected_Result: Exactly one item displays the selected/highlighted state.

Test_Objective: Verify hover effect appears on unselected department items
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Hover over an unselected department item.
Expected_Result: Item displays a subtle highlight effect. Cursor changes to pointer.

Test_Objective: Verify hover effect on the already selected department item
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Hover over the currently selected (highlighted) department item.
Expected_Result: Cursor changes to pointer. Selected highlight is maintained.

Test_Objective: Verify scrollbar appears when department list exceeds visible area
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Observe whether a vertical scrollbar is present.
Expected_Result: A vertical scrollbar is displayed since the list contains 50 departments but only ~6 are visible at once.

Test_Objective: Verify user can scroll to see all departments including the last item
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Scroll down to the bottom of the list.
Expected_Result: The last department in the list is visible and accessible. Scrolling is smooth.

Test_Objective: Verify user can scroll back to the top of the department list
Precondition: Dropdown is open, scrolled to bottom
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Scroll to the bottom of the list.
3. Scroll back to the top.
Expected_Result: The first department in the list is visible. Scrolling is smooth.

Test_Objective: Verify dropdown displays correctly on mobile viewport
Steps:
1. Set viewport to 320px width.
2. Open the department dropdown.
Expected_Result: Dropdown displays correctly without overflowing the screen. Items are readable and tappable (touch targets at least 44x44px).

Test_Objective: Verify dropdown displays correctly on tablet viewport
Steps:
1. Set viewport to 768px width.
2. Open the department dropdown.
Expected_Result: Dropdown displays correctly, properly positioned, items readable.

Test_Objective: Verify dropdown displays correctly on desktop viewport
Precondition: Browser viewport set to 1024px or wider
Steps:
1. Set viewport to 1024px width.
2. Open the department dropdown.
Expected_Result: Dropdown displays correctly as per design specs (101x348px container, dark background, rounded corners).

Test_Objective: Verify selecting the first department in the list works correctly
Precondition: Dropdown is open, no department currently selected (or different department selected)
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Click on the first department item in the list.
Expected_Result: Department is selected, dropdown closes, page filter is applied showing kudos for people in that department.

Test_Objective: Verify selecting a department from the middle of the list works correctly
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Scroll to a department in the middle of the list (e.g. CEVEC).
3. Click on that department item.
Expected_Result: Department is selected, dropdown closes, page filter is applied showing kudos for people in that department.

Test_Objective: Verify selecting the last department in the list works correctly
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Scroll to the bottom of the list.
3. Click on the last department item (PAO - PAO).
Expected_Result: Department is selected, dropdown closes, page filter is applied showing kudos for people in that department.

Test_Objective: Verify switching between departments updates the filter correctly
Precondition: A department is currently selected (e.g. CEVC2)
Steps:
1. Observe current filter results for CEVC2.
2. Open the department dropdown.
3. Select a different department (e.g. CEVC3).
Expected_Result: Dropdown closes. Page filter updates to show kudos for people in CEVC3. Previous CEVC2 filter is no longer applied. CEVC3 is now highlighted as selected.

Test_Objective: Verify selecting the already-selected department does not cause errors
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Click on CEVC2 (the already highlighted/selected department).
Expected_Result: Dropdown closes. Filter remains the same for CEVC2. No errors or unexpected behavior.

Test_Objective: Verify page content is filtered correctly for the selected department
Precondition: User is on the kudos page
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Select department CEVC2.
3. Observe the kudos displayed on the page.
Expected_Result: Only kudos addressed to people belonging to CEVC2 department are displayed.

Test_Objective: Verify behavior when selecting a department that has no associated kudos
Precondition: User is on the kudos page
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Select a department with no kudos (e.g. a newly created department).
Expected_Result: Page displays an empty state or "no results" message. No errors.

Test_Objective: Verify the department filter remains applied when interacting with other page elements
Steps:
1. Select a department (e.g. CEVC3).
2. Interact with other elements on the page (scroll, view details, etc.).
3. Observe whether the filter is still active.
Expected_Result: Department filter remains active. Content continues to show only kudos for the selected department.

Test_Objective: Verify all departments from the database are displayed in the dropdown
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Scroll through the entire list.
3. Count and verify all department names.
Expected_Result: All 50 departments are present in the list, matching the expected department data.

Test_Objective: Verify departments are listed in the correct order
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Scroll through and verify the order of departments.
Expected_Result: Departments are displayed in the expected order as specified in the design.

Test_Objective: Verify keyboard navigation through department items using arrow keys
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Press the Down arrow key multiple times.
3. Press the Up arrow key multiple times.
Expected_Result: Highlight moves to the next item on Down press and previous item on Up press. Highlight wraps or stops at boundaries.

Test_Objective: Verify pressing Enter selects the currently highlighted department
Precondition: Dropdown is open, an item is highlighted via keyboard
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Press Down arrow to highlight a different department.
3. Press Enter.
Expected_Result: Highlighted department is selected. Dropdown closes. Page filter is applied for the selected department.

Test_Objective: Verify that typing text in the dropdown does not input characters
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Type random characters on the keyboard (e.g. "abc").
Expected_Result: No text is entered or displayed in the dropdown. The dropdown remains functional.

Test_Objective: Verify scrolling through the department list with mouse wheel
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Use mouse wheel to scroll down through the list.
Expected_Result: List scrolls smoothly. All departments below the initial view become visible.

Test_Objective: Verify rapid scrolling through the department list does not cause issues
Steps:
1. Open the department dropdown.
2. Quickly scroll from top to bottom and back.
Expected_Result: Scrolling is smooth. Items render correctly without visual glitches. Dropdown remains responsive.

Test_Objective: Verify the dropdown reflects a newly added department after page reload
Precondition: Access to database to add a new department
Steps:
1. Add a new department to the database.
2. Reload the page.
3. Open the department dropdown.
Expected_Result: New department appears in the dropdown list. Dropdown operates normally.

Test_Objective: Verify the dropdown reflects a deleted department after page reload
Precondition: Access to database to delete a department
Steps:
1. Delete a department from the database.
2. Reload the page.
3. Open the department dropdown.
Expected_Result: Deleted department no longer appears in the dropdown list. Dropdown operates normally.

Test_Objective: Verify an updated department name is reflected in the dropdown after page reload
Precondition: Access to database to update a department name
Steps:
1. Update a department name in the database.
2. Reload the page.
3. Open the department dropdown.
Expected_Result: Updated department name is displayed correctly. Dropdown operates normally.

Test_Objective: Verify behavior when the currently selected department is deleted from DB without page reload
Precondition: A department is selected, then that department is deleted from DB
Steps:
1. Select a department (e.g. CEVC3).
2. Without reloading, delete CEVC3 from the database (via another session).
3. Interact with the page (e.g. attempt to use the filter).
Expected_Result: An appropriate error message is displayed or the page handles gracefully (e.g. resets to default).

Test_Objective: Verify server-side is not affected when dropdown value is changed to a non-existent value via browser DevTools
Precondition: Dropdown is open, user has browser DevTools access
Steps:
1. Open browser DevTools.
2. Inspect the dropdown element.
3. Change the selected value to a non-existent department ID.
4. Submit/apply the filter.
Expected_Result: Server rejects the invalid value or ignores it. Page function is unaffected. No server error.

Test_Objective: Verify server-side correctly processes when dropdown value is changed to an existing valid value via browser DevTools
Precondition: Dropdown is open, user has browser DevTools access
Steps:
1. Open browser DevTools.
2. Inspect the dropdown element.
3. Change the selected value to a different existing department ID.
4. Submit/apply the filter.
Expected_Result: Operation is unaffected by the client-side manipulation. Server validates and processes correctly.

Test_Objective: Verify adding a fake dropdown item via DevTools does not affect server operation
Precondition: Dropdown is open, user has browser DevTools access
Steps:
1. Open browser DevTools.
2. Add a new option element to the dropdown with a fabricated value.
3. Select the fabricated item.
4. Submit/apply the filter.
Expected_Result: Server rejects the fabricated value. Operation is unaffected. No data corruption.
